message:
fix: test infrastructure and additional bug fixes

## Test Fixes
- checkpoint.test.ts: Fix migration path for monorepo structure
- checkpoint.test.ts: Update ID regex for ULID format
- checkpoint.test.ts: Add delay between checkpoint creates for reliable ordering
- context.service.test.ts: Add nullish coalescing for model limits

## Checkpoint Service
- checkpoint.ts: Remove cache references (now pure DB queries)
- checkpoint.ts: Uses ULID for checkpoint IDs (external refactor)

## Context Routes
- context.ts: Remove unused getContextPackSummary import
- context.ts: Add removeUndefined helper for TypeScript strict mode
- context.ts: Make budget strategy schema fields optional

## Other
- tokenizer.service.ts: Remove unused ratio variable
- web/package.json: Add required dependencies

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-ogy","title":"EPIC: Phase 4 - Production Ready","description":"## Overview
Phase 4 polishes the system for production deployment with advanced analytics, comprehensive observability, performance optimization, and thorough testing.

## Phase 4 Goal
Polish, performance, reliability, and advanced analytics

## Key Deliverables

### Observability
- Metrics and alerts system
  - OpenTelemetry integration
  - Prometheus metrics export
  - Grafana dashboards
  - Alert rules and notification routing

### Analytics Dashboards
- Agent Performance Analytics
  - Model comparison (Claude vs Codex vs Gemini)
  - Productivity trends over time
  - Token usage patterns
  - Success/failure rates
  - AI-powered recommendations
- Cost Analytics & Optimization
  - Budget tracking per team/project
  - Forecasting based on trends
  - Cost optimization suggestions
  - Token usage breakdown
- Flywheel Velocity Dashboard
  - Ecosystem health metrics
  - Learning rate tracking
  - Cross-repo activity
  - Bottleneck identification
- Custom Dashboard Builder
  - Drag-and-drop widget placement
  - Personalized views per user
  - Saved layouts
  - Widget gallery

### Notifications
- Comprehensive Notification System
  - Multi-channel delivery (email, Slack, webhooks)
  - User preferences management
  - Smart digests
  - Notification batching
  - Do-not-disturb schedules

### Reliability
- Audit trail hardening
  - Tamper-evident logging
  - Exportable audit reports
  - Configurable retention
  - Full-text search
- Pipeline engine
  - Multi-step workflows
  - Conditional execution
  - Retry policies
  - Pipeline templates

### User Experience
- Mobile optimization
  - Responsive design
  - Touch-friendly interactions
  - Progressive Web App (PWA)
  - Offline mode for viewing

### Performance
- Performance optimization
  - WebSocket backpressure handling
  - Output virtualization for long streams
  - Database query optimization
  - Caching strategies

### Quality Assurance
- Comprehensive testing
  - Unit tests (>80% coverage)
  - Integration tests
  - Contract tests (OpenAPI)
  - E2E tests (Playwright)
  - Load tests (k6)
  - Visual regression tests

### Documentation
- Complete documentation
  - API reference (from OpenAPI)
  - User guides
  - Developer guides
  - Deployment guides
  - Runbooks

## Phase Completion Criteria
- [ ] OpenTelemetry metrics exported to Prometheus
- [ ] Grafana dashboards configured and working
- [ ] Agent Performance Analytics shows model comparison
- [ ] Cost Analytics tracks and forecasts usage
- [ ] Velocity Dashboard shows ecosystem health
- [ ] Custom dashboards can be created and saved
- [ ] Notifications delivered via all channels
- [ ] Audit exports generate compliance reports
- [ ] Pipeline engine runs multi-step workflows
- [ ] Mobile UI is fully functional
- [ ] All performance targets met (see non-functional requirements)
- [ ] All test suites passing in CI
- [ ] Documentation complete and published

## Testing Requirements
- Full test coverage at all levels
- Load testing passes all performance targets
- Security audit completed
- Accessibility audit (WCAG AA)
- Cross-browser testing (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
- Mobile device testing (iOS, Android)

### Logging
- [ ] Unit/integration tests emit structured logs with `correlationId` + relevant entity IDs (never secrets)
- [ ] E2E tests capture artifacts (trace/video/screenshot/console logs) and surface failure context per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`


## Success Criteria

- [ ] Observability complete: metrics + alerts + dashboards + durable audit export with redaction
- [ ] Performance targets met for core paths (WS backpressure, output virtualization, query latency)
- [ ] Mobile UX and accessibility targets met for critical workflows
- [ ] Pipeline engine production-ready: validate/execute/resume workflows with approvals and rich diagnostics
- [ ] Test suite is comprehensive and automated (unit + integration + contract + E2E + load) with artifact capture and structured test logging per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`

## Reference

- `flywheel_gateway-y19` (embedded `docs/PLAN.md` spec snapshot)

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-p0l","title":"FEAT: SLB Safety Guardrails","description":"## Overview

Configurable safety limits and approval workflows for the Session Lock Broker (SLB). Safety Guardrails provide multi-layered protection against runaway agents, resource abuse, and potentially dangerous operations through declarative rules, rate limiting, and human-in-the-loop approval workflows.

## Background & Reasoning

Autonomous agents operating with tool access pose inherent risks:

- **Runaway Agents**: An agent stuck in a loop can consume unlimited resources or make endless API calls
- **Resource Abuse**: Without limits, agents can exhaust disk space, network bandwidth, or compute time
- **Dangerous Operations**: Some operations (forced pushes, recursive deletion, production deployments) require human oversight
- **Cost Overruns**: LLM token usage and external API calls can generate unexpected expenses
- **Data Exfiltration**: Agents with network access could potentially leak sensitive information

Safety Guardrails transform these risks into manageable, auditable controls with clear escalation paths.

## Technical Architecture

### Safety Categories

| Category | Scope | Examples |
|----------|-------|----------|
| `filesystem` | File and directory operations | Path restrictions, write limits, deletion controls |
| `git` | Version control operations | Branch protections, forced push blocking, commit limits |
| `network` | External communication | URL allowlists, request rate limits, data transfer caps |
| `execution` | Command and script execution | Command blocking, timeout enforcement, process limits |
| `resources` | System resource usage | Memory caps, CPU limits, disk quotas |
| `content` | Generated content validation | PII detection, secret scanning, policy compliance |

### Path Allowlists and Denylists

```typescript
interface PathRule {
  pattern: string;      // Glob pattern (e.g., '/workspace/**', '!**/node_modules/**')
  action: 'allow' | 'deny';
  operations: ('read' | 'write' | 'delete' | 'execute')[];
  reason?: string;      // Displayed to agent when blocked
}

// Evaluation order: deny rules checked first, then allow rules
// Default: deny if no matching allow rule
```

### Command Blocking Rules

```typescript
interface CommandRule {
  pattern: string;           // Regex pattern for command matching
  severity: 'block' | 'warn' | 'approve';
  category: SafetyCategory;
  reason: string;
  alternatives?: string[];   // Suggested safer alternatives
}

// Example rules:
// - block: recursive forced deletion from root
// - approve: forced git push (requires approval)
// - warn: piping curl output to shell
```

### Rate Limiting

```typescript
interface RateLimitConfig {
  scope: 'agent' | 'workspace' | 'session';
  limits: {
    tokensPerMinute: number;
    requestsPerMinute: number;
    fileWritesPerMinute: number;
    networkRequestsPerMinute: number;
    commandsPerMinute: number;
  };
  burstAllowance: number;    // Percentage above limit for short bursts
  cooldownSeconds: number;   // Wait time after limit exceeded
}
```

### Approval Queue

```typescript
interface ApprovalRequest {
  id: string;
  agentId: string;
  sessionId: string;
  operation: {
    type: SafetyCategory;
    command?: string;
    path?: string;
    description: string;
  };
  rule: SafetyRule;          // Rule that triggered approval
  context: {
    recentActions: Action[]; // Last 10 agent actions for context
    taskDescription?: string;
  };
  status: 'pending' | 'approved' | 'denied' | 'expired';
  requestedAt: Date;
  expiresAt: Date;           // Auto-deny after timeout
  decidedBy?: string;        // User who approved/denied
  decidedAt?: Date;
  decisionReason?: string;
}
```

### Cost Limits and Budget Enforcement

```typescript
interface BudgetConfig {
  scope: 'agent' | 'workspace' | 'session';
  limits: {
    totalTokens: number;
    totalDollars: number;
    perRequestDollars: number;
  };
  alertThresholds: number[]; // e.g., [0.5, 0.8, 0.95] for 50%, 80%, 95%
  action: 'warn' | 'pause' | 'terminate';
}
```

## Key Interfaces

```typescript
interface SafetyConfig {
  id: string;
  workspaceId: string;
  name: string;
  description?: string;
  enabled: boolean;
  categories: {
    [K in SafetyCategory]: {
      enabled: boolean;
      rules: SafetyRule[];
    };
  };
  rateLimits: RateLimitConfig;
  budget: BudgetConfig;
  approvalWorkflow: {
    enabled: boolean;
    approvers: string[];      // User IDs who can approve
    timeoutMinutes: number;
    defaultAction: 'deny' | 'allow';
  };
}

interface SafetyRule {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  category: SafetyCategory;
  condition: RuleCondition;   // Pattern matching logic
  action: 'allow' | 'deny' | 'warn' | 'approve';
  severity: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | 'critical';
  message: string;            // Shown to agent/user
  enabled: boolean;
}

interface SafetyViolation {
  id: string;
  timestamp: Date;
  agentId: string;
  sessionId: string;
  rule: SafetyRule;
  operation: {
    type: string;
    details: Record<string, unknown>;
  };
  action: 'blocked' | 'warned' | 'pending_approval';
  context: {
    taskDescription?: string;
    recentHistory: string[];
  };
}
```

## File Locations

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| Safety service | `apps/gateway/src/services/safety.service.ts` |
| Safety rules engine | `apps/gateway/src/services/safety-rules.engine.ts` |
| Rate limiter | `apps/gateway/src/services/rate-limiter.service.ts` |
| Approval service | `apps/gateway/src/services/approval.service.ts` |
| Safety config API | `apps/gateway/src/routes/safety.routes.ts` |
| Safety config UI | `apps/web/src/components/safety/SafetyConfigEditor.tsx` |
| Approval queue UI | `apps/web/src/components/safety/ApprovalQueue.tsx` |
| Violation log UI | `apps/web/src/components/safety/ViolationLog.tsx` |
| Safety tests | `apps/gateway/src/services/__tests__/safety.*.test.ts` |

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Rule matching engine correctly evaluates glob patterns
- [ ] Rule matching engine correctly evaluates regex patterns
- [ ] Rule precedence (deny before allow) works correctly
- [ ] Rate limiter tracks counts per scope accurately
- [ ] Rate limiter resets after cooldown period
- [ ] Budget calculations handle currency precision
- [ ] Approval expiration triggers correct action

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Approval workflow sends notifications to approvers
- [ ] Approved operations execute successfully
- [ ] Denied operations return appropriate error to agent
- [ ] Rate limit exceeded pauses agent correctly
- [ ] Budget alert triggers at configured thresholds
- [ ] Config changes apply to active sessions

### E2E Tests
- [ ] Blocked command shows user-friendly error message
- [ ] Approval request appears in queue within 1 second
- [ ] Approver can approve/deny from queue UI
- [ ] Agent receives approval result and continues
- [ ] Violation log shows all blocked operations
- [ ] Safety config changes persist across restarts

### Security Tests
- [ ] Path traversal attempts blocked (../../etc/passwd)
- [ ] Glob pattern injection prevented
- [ ] Regex ReDoS patterns detected and rejected
- [ ] Approval bypass attempts logged and blocked
- [ ] Rate limit bypass attempts (clock manipulation) fail
- [ ] Budget manipulation attempts fail

### Logging
- [ ] Guardrail tests log `policyId`, `approvalId`, action fingerprint, and decision outcome with correlation IDs
- [ ] Audit trail validates no sensitive command args are stored unredacted


## Logging Requirements

### Safety Decisions
```typescript
logger.info('safety.decision', {
  agentId,
  operation,
  rule: rule.id,
  action: 'blocked' | 'allowed' | 'pending',
  reason: rule.message,
  context: { taskDescription, recentActions }
});
```

### Approval Audit Trail
```typescript
logger.info('safety.approval.requested', { requestId, agentId, operation });
logger.info('safety.approval.decided', { 
  requestId, 
  decision: 'approved' | 'denied',
  decidedBy,
  reason 
});
logger.warn('safety.approval.expired', { requestId, operation });
```

### Violation Metrics
```typescript
// Emitted for dashboards and alerting
metrics.increment('safety.violations', { 
  category, 
  severity, 
  action,
  workspaceId 
});
metrics.gauge('safety.approval_queue_depth', queueSize, { workspaceId });
metrics.histogram('safety.approval_latency', latencyMs, { decision });
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Default safety config blocks known dangerous operations
- [ ] Custom rules can be added via UI without code changes
- [ ] Path allowlist/denylist supports standard glob patterns
- [ ] Rate limiting pauses agent without losing context
- [ ] Budget alerts notify workspace admins at thresholds
- [ ] Approval queue shows pending requests with full context
- [ ] Approvals complete within configured timeout
- [ ] All safety decisions logged with complete audit trail
- [ ] Violation dashboard shows trends and top blocked operations
- [ ] Safety config can be exported/imported for environment parity
- [ ] Emergency kill switch terminates agent immediately
- [ ] Safety rules evaluated in < 5ms per operation

## Reference

PLAN.md §17 - SLB Safety Guardrails

- [ ] Approver unavailable: escalate","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-08T13:48:15.850431776-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T20:50:00.095900145-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_gateway-p0l","depends_on_id":"flywheel_gateway-41h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T14:02:00.315942206-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-p8j","title":"FEAT: Beads/BV Integration","description":"## Background

The Beads Viewer (BV) is the central intelligence layer of the Flywheel ecosystem, managing the dependency graph of all work items (beads). This integration enables Flywheel Gateway to leverage BV's graph-aware capabilities for intelligent work prioritization and bottleneck detection.

## Reasoning

Current task management systems treat work items as independent entities, leading to:
- Developers picking up blocked work they cannot complete
- Critical path items languishing while non-blocking work proceeds
- No visibility into which completions would unblock the most downstream work
- Manual effort to determine work dependencies

BV solves this by maintaining a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of beads and their dependencies, enabling queries like "what can I work on right now?" and "what's blocking the most work?"

## Technical Considerations

### Client Architecture
- Use flywheel-clients package pattern for consistency
- Implement retry logic with exponential backoff
- Cache dependency graph locally with invalidation strategy
- Support both REST and potential WebSocket for real-time updates

### API Design
```typescript
interface BVClient {
  // CRUD Operations
  createBead(bead: BeadCreate): Promise<Bead>;
  getBead(id: string): Promise<Bead>;
  updateBead(id: string, updates: BeadUpdate): Promise<Bead>;
  deleteBead(id: string): Promise<void>;
  
  // Triage Operations
  getReadyBeads(filters?: TriageFilters): Promise<Bead[]>;
  getBlockedBeads(filters?: TriageFilters): Promise<BlockedBead[]>;
  triageWork(context: TriageContext): Promise<PrioritizedWork>;
  
  // Insights
  getBottlenecks(limit?: number): Promise<Bottleneck[]>;
  getKeystones(limit?: number): Promise<Keystone[]>;
  getDependencyGraph(rootId?: string): Promise<DependencyGraph>;
  
  // Dependency Management
  addDependency(beadId: string, dependsOn: string): Promise<void>;
  removeDependency(beadId: string, dependsOn: string): Promise<void>;
  getBlockers(beadId: string): Promise<Bead[]>;
  getDependents(beadId: string): Promise<Bead[]>;
}
```

### Galaxy View (Dependency Visualization)
The Galaxy View renders the dependency graph as an interactive visualization:
- Nodes represent beads, sized by downstream impact
- Edges show dependency relationships
- Color coding: ready (green), blocked (red), in-progress (yellow)
- Zoom/pan for large graphs
- Click to drill into bead details
- Filter by project, tag, assignee

### Performance Requirements
- Triage queries must return in <100ms for responsive UI
- Graph rendering must handle 1000+ nodes smoothly
- Use WebGL (via @xyflow/react (ReactFlow) or similar) for large graphs

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **BV Client Implementation**
   - [ ] BVClient class with full TypeScript typing
   - [ ] All CRUD operations functional
   - [ ] Error handling with typed exceptions
   - [ ] Request/response logging for debugging
   - [ ] Unit tests with >80% coverage

2. **Triage API Integration**
   - [ ] getReadyBeads returns beads with no unresolved blockers
   - [ ] getBlockedBeads includes blocker information
   - [ ] triageWork returns prioritized list considering:
     - User skills/preferences
     - Bead priority
     - Downstream impact (keystone score)
     - Time estimates

3. **Insights API Integration**
   - [ ] Bottleneck detection working (beads blocking most downstream work)
   - [ ] Keystone identification (highest-impact completions)
   - [ ] Metrics include: blocked count, estimated unblock value

4. **Dependency Management**
   - [ ] Add/remove dependencies with cycle detection
   - [ ] Bulk dependency operations
   - [ ] Dependency validation (no self-references, no cycles)

5. **Galaxy View Component**
   - [ ] Interactive graph rendering
   - [ ] Node click shows bead details sidebar
   - [ ] Filter controls (status, project, assignee)
   - [ ] Legend explaining visual encoding
   - [ ] Export graph as image/SVG
   - [ ] Responsive design for different screen sizes

6. **Integration Tests**
   - [ ] End-to-end flow: create bead -> add dependency -> triage -> complete
   - [ ] Graph updates reflect in Galaxy View within 2 seconds

## File Locations

### Client Package
- `packages/flywheel-clients/src/bv/index.ts` - Main exports
- `packages/flywheel-clients/src/bv/client.ts` - BVClient implementation
- `packages/flywheel-clients/src/bv/types.ts` - TypeScript interfaces
- `packages/flywheel-clients/src/bv/errors.ts` - Error classes
- `packages/flywheel-clients/src/bv/__tests__/` - Unit tests

### Web Components
- `apps/web/src/components/beads/GalaxyView.tsx` - Main graph visualization
- `apps/web/src/components/beads/BeadNode.tsx` - Individual node component
- `apps/web/src/components/beads/BeadSidebar.tsx` - Detail sidebar
- `apps/web/src/components/beads/TriagePanel.tsx` - Triage interface
- `apps/web/src/components/beads/InsightsPanel.tsx` - Bottlenecks/keystones display
- `apps/web/src/components/beads/DependencyEditor.tsx` - Manage dependencies
- `apps/web/src/hooks/useBVClient.ts` - React hook for BV operations
- `apps/web/src/hooks/useTriageData.ts` - Hook for triage queries

## References

- PLAN.md §13: Beads/BV Integration specifications
- BV API documentation (internal)
- @xyflow/react (ReactFlow) library for visualization


## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] BV adapter parses `--robot-*` JSON output into validated schemas
- [ ] Cache TTL and invalidation behavior is correct (e.g., after `bd sync`)

### Integration Tests
- [ ] When bv is installed (or mocked): triage/plan/priority endpoints return expected structure

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] bv missing / no baseline / timeout → actionable errors and degraded UI state

### E2E Tests
- [ ] Covered by `flywheel_gateway-tz4` critical-path E2E suite; add a focused E2E spec if this feature introduces unique user workflows
- [ ] Failure mode E2E validates actionable recovery UI/messages (with artifacts captured)

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + bvCommand + dataHash + latencyMs



## Implementation Notes (bv CLI)

- Gateway must never run bare `bv` (interactive TUI). Use ONLY `bv --robot-*` flags and parse their JSON output.
- Prefer `bv --robot-triage` as the primary planning entrypoint; use `bv --robot-next` for a single next item; use `bv --robot-plan`/`--robot-insights` for deeper graph analysis.
- Failure modes (missing binary, non-zero exit, timeout, malformed JSON) must map to actionable GatewayError codes and a degraded-but-stable UI state.

","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- [ ] BVClient.createBead: sends correct request
- [ ] BVClient.getBead: parses response correctly
- [ ] BVClient.updateBead: sends PATCH request
- [ ] BVClient.deleteBead: sends DELETE request
- [ ] BVClient.getReadyBeads: applies filters
- [ ] BVClient.getBlockedBeads: includes blocker info
- [ ] BVClient.triageWork: returns prioritized list
- [ ] BVClient.getBottlenecks: returns blocking beads
- [ ] BVClient.getKeystones: returns high-impact beads
- [ ] BVClient.getDependencyGraph: builds graph structure
- [ ] BVClient.addDependency: validates no cycles
- [ ] BVClient.removeDependency: removes edge
- [ ] Retry logic: exponential backoff
- [ ] Cache: stores graph with TTL
- [ ] Cache: invalidates on mutation

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Create bead via client
- [ ] Get ready beads filters correctly
- [ ] Add dependency creates edge
- [ ] Cycle detection prevents invalid dependency
- [ ] Triage returns scored recommendations
- [ ] Bottleneck detection finds blocking beads
- [ ] Graph endpoint returns valid structure

### E2E Tests
- [ ] Full flow: create -> add dep -> triage -> complete
- [ ] Galaxy View renders graph from API
- [ ] Node click shows bead details
- [ ] Filter controls update view
- [ ] Graph updates within 2 seconds of change

### Performance Tests
- [ ] Triage query <100ms
- [ ] Graph render 1000 nodes smooth (60fps)
- [ ] Graph render 5000 nodes acceptable (30fps)
- [ ] Cache hit <10ms

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] BV unavailable: graceful degradation
- [ ] Invalid bead ID: 404 response
- [ ] Cycle in dependency: rejected with explanation
- [ ] Large graph: progressive loading","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-08T13:45:58.967402792-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T02:38:31.852341415-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_gateway-p8j","depends_on_id":"flywheel_gateway-45c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T14:01:46.338132749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_gateway-r3p","title":"FEAT: Basic Web UI Shell with Mock-Data Mode","description":"## Background

The Web UI Shell provides the foundational application container for the Flywheel Gateway dashboard. It establishes the visual framework, routing infrastructure, global state management, and component library that all features build upon.

### Why Phase 1 Foundation?
- **Validates Stack**: Proves Vite + React 19 + TanStack + Tailwind 4 work together
- **Mock-Data Mode**: Allows frontend development without backend dependency
- **Design System**: Establishes patterns that all future components follow
- **Integration Points**: Sets up WebSocket provider, query client, error boundaries

## Technical Architecture

### Technology Stack
- **Build**: Vite 7.3+ with React Compiler plugin
- **Framework**: React 19.2+ with Suspense boundaries
- **Routing**: TanStack Router 1.145+ (type-safe, file-based)
- **Data**: TanStack Query 5.90+ for server state
- **State**: Zustand for client state
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4.1+ (CSS-based config)
- **Animation**: Framer Motion for transitions
- **Icons**: Lucide React (consistent iconography)

### Application Shell Structure

```typescript
// apps/web/src/App.tsx
function App() {
  return (
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
      <RouterProvider router={router} />
      <WebSocketProvider>
        <Toaster />
      </WebSocketProvider>
    </QueryClientProvider>
  );
}

// Layout structure
function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <div className="flex h-screen">
      <Sidebar />
      <div className="flex flex-col flex-1">
        <Topbar />
        <main className="flex-1 overflow-auto p-6">
          <Suspense fallback={<PageSkeleton />}>
            {children}
          </Suspense>
        </main>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Design System Foundation

#### CSS Custom Properties
```css
/* apps/web/src/styles/design-tokens.css */
:root {
  /* Colors - Semantic */
  --color-background: 0 0% 100%;
  --color-foreground: 222.2 84% 4.9%;
  --color-primary: 222.2 47.4% 11.2%;
  --color-primary-foreground: 210 40% 98%;
  --color-muted: 210 40% 96.1%;
  --color-muted-foreground: 215.4 16.3% 46.9%;
  --color-accent: 210 40% 96.1%;
  --color-destructive: 0 84.2% 60.2%;
  --color-border: 214.3 31.8% 91.4%;
  --color-ring: 222.2 84% 4.9%;
  
  /* Agent Status Colors */
  --color-agent-spawning: 38 92% 50%;
  --color-agent-ready: 142 76% 36%;
  --color-agent-executing: 217 91% 60%;
  --color-agent-paused: 38 92% 50%;
  --color-agent-terminated: 0 0% 45%;
  --color-agent-failed: 0 84% 60%;
  
  /* Spacing */
  --spacing-xs: 0.25rem;
  --spacing-sm: 0.5rem;
  --spacing-md: 1rem;
  --spacing-lg: 1.5rem;
  --spacing-xl: 2rem;
  
  /* Radii */
  --radius-sm: 0.25rem;
  --radius-md: 0.375rem;
  --radius-lg: 0.5rem;
  
  /* Shadows */
  --shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05);
  --shadow-md: 0 4px 6px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1);
  --shadow-lg: 0 10px 15px -3px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1);
}

.dark {
  --color-background: 222.2 84% 4.9%;
  --color-foreground: 210 40% 98%;
  /* ... dark mode overrides */
}
```

### Core Components

#### Sidebar Navigation
```typescript
interface NavItem {
  label: string;
  icon: LucideIcon;
  href: string;
  badge?: number;
  children?: NavItem[];
}

const navigation: NavItem[] = [
  { label: 'Dashboard', icon: LayoutDashboard, href: '/' },
  { label: 'Agents', icon: Bot, href: '/agents', badge: activeAgentCount },
  { label: 'Pipelines', icon: GitBranch, href: '/pipelines' },
  { label: 'Beads', icon: CircleDot, href: '/beads' },
  { label: 'Memory', icon: Brain, href: '/memory' },
  { label: 'Fleet', icon: Server, href: '/fleet' },
  { label: 'Settings', icon: Settings, href: '/settings' },
];
```

#### Command Palette (Cmd+K)
```typescript
interface CommandPaletteAction {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  icon?: LucideIcon;
  shortcut?: string;
  action: () => void | Promise<void>;
  group: 'navigation' | 'agents' | 'actions' | 'settings';
}
```

### Mock-Data Mode

```typescript
// apps/web/src/lib/mock-data.ts
export const MOCK_MODE = import.meta.env.VITE_MOCK_DATA === 'true';

// TanStack Query with mock interceptor
const queryClient = new QueryClient({
  defaultOptions: {
    queries: {
      queryFn: MOCK_MODE ? mockQueryFn : undefined,
      staleTime: MOCK_MODE ? Infinity : 5000,
    },
  },
});

// Mock data fixtures
export const mockAgents: Agent[] = [
  { id: 'agent-1', name: 'Claude', status: 'ready', ... },
  { id: 'agent-2', name: 'Codex', status: 'executing', ... },
];

export const mockBeads: Bead[] = [...];
export const mockPipelines: Pipeline[] = [...];
```

### Error Boundaries

```typescript
// Global error boundary with correlation ID preservation
function ErrorBoundary({ error }: { error: Error }) {
  const correlationId = error.cause?.correlationId;
  
  return (
    <div className="error-container">
      <h2>Something went wrong</h2>
      <p>{error.message}</p>
      {correlationId && (
        <p className="text-muted-foreground">
          Reference: {correlationId}
        </p>
      )}
      <Button onClick={() => window.location.reload()}>
        Reload Page
      </Button>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Responsive Design

```typescript
// Breakpoint constants
const breakpoints = {
  sm: '640px',
  md: '768px',
  lg: '1024px',
  xl: '1280px',
  '2xl': '1536px',
};

// useMediaQuery hook
function useMediaQuery(query: string): boolean {
  const [matches, setMatches] = useState(false);
  
  useEffect(() => {
    const media = window.matchMedia(query);
    setMatches(media.matches);
    const listener = () => setMatches(media.matches);
    media.addEventListener('change', listener);
    return () => media.removeEventListener('change', listener);
  }, [query]);
  
  return matches;
}

// Mobile sidebar collapses to hamburger menu
// Tablet shows collapsed icon-only sidebar
// Desktop shows full sidebar with labels
```

## File Locations

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `apps/web/src/App.tsx` | Root application component |
| `apps/web/src/components/layout/` | Shell components (Sidebar, Topbar, etc.) |
| `apps/web/src/components/ui/` | Design system primitives |
| `apps/web/src/lib/mock-data.ts` | Mock data fixtures |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/` | Shared hooks (useMediaQuery, useDarkMode) |
| `apps/web/src/styles/` | CSS files including design tokens |
| `apps/web/src/routes/` | TanStack Router route definitions |

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- [ ] AppShell renders correctly with sidebar and topbar
- [ ] Navigation links highlight correctly based on route
- [ ] Mock data mode returns expected fixtures
- [ ] Dark mode toggle persists preference
- [ ] Responsive breakpoint hooks return correct values

### Component Tests
- [ ] Button variants render with correct styles
- [ ] Modal opens/closes with animation
- [ ] Toast notifications appear and dismiss
- [ ] Command palette opens with Cmd+K

### E2E Tests (Playwright)
- [ ] User can navigate between all main routes
- [ ] Page loads within 2 seconds (Core Web Vitals)
- [ ] Mobile navigation works correctly
- [ ] Error boundary catches and displays errors gracefully
- [ ] Mock data mode shows all screens correctly
- [ ] Dark mode toggle switches theme without flash

### Accessibility Tests
- [ ] All interactive elements are keyboard accessible
- [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA standards
- [ ] Screen reader announces page changes

### Visual Regression Tests
- [ ] All pages match baseline screenshots (desktop + mobile)
- [ ] Component library matches design system

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Router + Query providers integrate with mock WS provider and fixture data
- [ ] Global error boundary/toast surfaces correlation IDs when provided

### Logging
- [ ] UI/E2E logs include route transitions + correlationId propagation + screenshot/trace on failure
- [ ] Error boundary/toast tests log rendered error codes (not raw stack traces with secrets)


## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Application shell exists with navigation, routing, and global providers (TanStack Router/Query + WS provider + Zustand)
- [ ] Mock-data mode is first-class (toggleable) and exercises all core screens without backend
- [ ] UI components follow the design system tokens and are accessible (keyboard + ARIA + contrast)
- [ ] Global error boundaries/toasts render actionable messages and preserve correlation IDs when present
- [ ] Responsive layout works on desktop and mobile breakpoints (no horizontal scroll)
- [ ] Command palette (Cmd+K) provides quick navigation
- [ ] Dark mode toggle works with smooth transition

## Reference

- PLAN.md §22 - Web UI Layer
- PLAN.md §22.1 - Design System
- PLAN.md §22.3 - Application Shell

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-rgx","title":"FEAT: Cost Analytics and Optimization","description":"## Background

AI agent operations represent a significant and growing cost center for organizations. Without proper cost visibility and optimization, expenses can spiral unexpectedly. This bead implements comprehensive cost analytics that provide real-time visibility into AI usage, predictive forecasting to prevent budget overruns, and AI-powered optimization recommendations to reduce costs while maintaining quality.

## Reasoning

Cost management for AI agents is uniquely challenging because:
- **Variable Rates**: Different models have vastly different costs (GPT-4 vs Claude Haiku)
- **Usage Patterns**: Costs vary by time of day, task type, and agent behavior
- **Hidden Costs**: Retries, context overflow, and inefficient prompts inflate costs
- **Rapid Growth**: Agent fleets can scale quickly, multiplying costs

The solution must provide:
- Granular cost attribution to understand where resources goes
- Proactive alerts before budgets are exhausted
- Actionable recommendations with quantified savings
- Historical analysis to identify cost trends and anomalies

## Technical Considerations

### Cost Tracking Architecture

**Cost Attribution Dimensions:**
```typescript
interface CostRecord {
  timestamp: Date;
  organization_id: string;
  project_id: string;
  agent_id: string;
  task_id: string;
  model: string;
  provider: 'anthropic' | 'openai' | 'google' | 'local';
  
  // Token breakdown
  prompt_tokens: number;
  completion_tokens: number;
  cached_tokens: number;
  
  // Cost calculation
  prompt_cost_units: number;
  completion_cost_units: number;
  total_cost_units: number;
  
  // Context
  task_type: string;
  complexity_tier: 'simple' | 'moderate' | 'complex';
  success: boolean;
}
```

**Real-time Cost Aggregation:**
- Per-minute cost accumulation for real-time dashboards
- Hourly rollups for trend analysis
- Daily aggregates for reporting
- Monthly summaries for reporting

**Cost by Model:**
```typescript
interface ModelCostBreakdown {
  model: string;
  total_cost_units: number;
  percentage_of_total: number;
  request_count: number;
  avg_cost_per_request: number;
  total_tokens: number;
  cost_per_1k_tokens: number;
  trend: CostTrend;
}
```

**Cost by Agent:**
- Individual agent cost tracking
- Agent cost ranking (top spenders)
- Cost per successful task by agent
- Agent efficiency comparison

**Cost by Task Type:**
- Task classification for cost attribution
- Cost benchmarks by task type
- Anomaly detection for expensive tasks
- Task type optimization opportunities

### Budget Status Visualization

**Budget Configuration:**
```typescript
interface Budget {
  id: string;
  organization_id: string;
  period: 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly';
  amount_units: number;
  alert_thresholds: number[]; // e.g., [50, 75, 90, 100]
  action_on_exceed: 'alert' | 'throttle' | 'block';
  rollover: boolean;
  effective_date: Date;
}
```

**Visual Progress Indicators:**
- Circular progress gauge (0-100%)
- Color coding: green (<50%), yellow (50-80%), orange (80-95%), red (>95%)
- Burn rate indicator (current vs expected)
- Days remaining at current rate
- Comparison to previous period

**Budget Alerts:**
- Threshold-based notifications
- Predictive alerts ("Will exceed budget in 3 days")
- Spike detection ("Cost increased 300% in last hour")
- Recovery notification when usage normalizes

### 30-Day Cost Forecasting

**Forecasting Model:**
```typescript
interface CostForecast {
  forecast_date: Date;
  horizon_days: 30;
  daily_forecasts: DailyForecast[];
  total_forecast_units: number;
  confidence_interval_95: {
    lower: number;
    upper: number;
  };
  methodology: 'arima' | 'prophet' | 'linear' | 'ensemble';
  accuracy_metrics: {
    mape: number; // Mean Absolute Percentage Error
    rmse: number; // Root Mean Square Error
  };
}

interface DailyForecast {
  date: Date;
  predicted_cost_units: number;
  lower_bound_units: number;
  upper_bound_units: number;
  confidence: number;
}
```

**Forecasting Methodology:**
1. **Historical Analysis**: Use 90 days of historical data
2. **Seasonality Detection**: Weekly and monthly patterns
3. **Trend Extraction**: Linear and exponential trends
4. **Anomaly Handling**: Exclude outliers from training
5. **Ensemble Approach**: Combine multiple models for robustness

**Confidence Intervals:**
- 95% confidence intervals for each day
- Widening intervals for further predictions
- Scenario modeling (optimistic, expected, pessimistic)
- Sensitivity analysis for key drivers

### AI-Powered Optimization Recommendations

**Recommendation Categories:**

1. **Model Optimization:**
```typescript
{
  category: 'model_optimization',
  title: 'Switch documentation tasks to Claude Haiku',
  description: 'Analysis shows 847 documentation tasks last month used Claude Sonnet. These tasks have 98% success rate with Haiku at 1/10th the cost.',
  current_cost: 423.50,
  optimized_cost: 42.35,
  estimated_savings: 381.15,
  confidence: 0.92,
  implementation: 'Update agent configuration to route doc tasks to haiku',
  risk: 'low'
}
```

2. **Caching Optimization:**
```typescript
{
  category: 'caching',
  title: 'Enable prompt caching for repetitive system prompts',
  description: 'Detected 12,847 requests with identical 4k token system prompts. Prompt caching would reduce costs by 75% for these tokens.',
  estimated_savings: 156.32,
  implementation: 'Enable cache_control in API calls'
}
```

3. **Batching Optimization:**
```typescript
{
  category: 'batching',
  title: 'Batch code review requests during off-peak hours',
  description: 'Non-urgent code reviews can be batched and processed at 50% discount using batch API.',
  estimated_savings: 89.50,
  implementation: 'Configure batch queue for code review tasks'
}
```

4. **Context Optimization:**
```typescript
{
  category: 'context_optimization',
  title: 'Reduce context size for simple queries',
  description: 'Simple queries averaging 2k completion tokens include 50k context. Reducing to 10k relevant context saves tokens.',
  estimated_savings: 234.00,
  implementation: 'Improve context selection algorithm'
}
```

5. **Agent Consolidation:**
```typescript
{
  category: 'consolidation',
  title: 'Consolidate low-utilization agents',
  description: '5 agents have <10% utilization. Consolidating to 2 agents reduces fixed overhead costs.',
  estimated_savings: 45.00,
  implementation: 'Migrate workloads and retire agents'
}
```

**Savings Estimation:**
- Historical data analysis for accuracy
- Conservative estimates (lower bound of range)
- Quality impact assessment
- Implementation effort consideration
- ROI calculation with payback period

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Cost Tracking**
   - [ ] All API calls tracked with full cost attribution
   - [ ] Real-time cost aggregation (< 1 minute delay)
   - [ ] Cost breakdown by model, agent, task type available
   - [ ] Historical cost data retained for 13 months

2. **Budget Management**
   - [ ] Budget configuration per organization/project
   - [ ] Visual progress with color-coded thresholds
   - [ ] Alert notifications at configured thresholds
   - [ ] Budget enforcement actions (throttle/block)

3. **Forecasting**
   - [ ] 30-day forecast with daily granularity
   - [ ] 95% confidence intervals displayed
   - [ ] Forecast accuracy tracking (MAPE < 15%)
   - [ ] Scenario analysis available

4. **Optimization Recommendations**
   - [ ] Automated recommendation generation weekly
   - [ ] Savings estimates with confidence levels
   - [ ] Implementation guidance provided
   - [ ] Recommendation outcome tracking

5. **Dashboard UI**
   - [ ] Cost overview with key metrics
   - [ ] Interactive charts with drill-down
   - [ ] Budget gauges with status indicators
   - [ ] Recommendation cards with actions

## File Locations

### Backend Services
- `apps/gateway/src/services/cost-analytics.service.ts` - Core cost analytics
- `apps/gateway/src/services/cost-tracker.service.ts` - Real-time cost tracking
- `apps/gateway/src/services/budget.service.ts` - Budget management
- `apps/gateway/src/services/cost-forecast.service.ts` - Forecasting engine
- `apps/gateway/src/services/cost-optimization.service.ts` - Recommendation generation
- `apps/gateway/src/controllers/cost-analytics.controller.ts` - Cost API endpoints

### Database
- `packages/database/prisma/migrations/xxx_add_cost_analytics.sql` - Schema
- Tables: `cost_records`, `cost_aggregates_hourly`, `cost_aggregates_daily`, `budgets`, `cost_forecasts`, `optimization_recommendations`

### Frontend Components
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/CostDashboard.tsx` - Main cost dashboard
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/CostBreakdownChart.tsx` - Cost by dimension
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/BudgetGauge.tsx` - Budget progress indicator
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/CostForecastChart.tsx` - Forecast visualization
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/OptimizationRecommendations.tsx` - Savings suggestions
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/CostTrendChart.tsx` - Historical trends

### Configuration
- `apps/gateway/src/config/cost-model.config.ts` - Model cost coefficients
- `apps/gateway/src/config/cost-thresholds.config.ts` - Alert thresholds

## References

- PLAN.md §21.6 - Cost Analytics and Optimization
- Model rate coefficients are configured externally (do not embed provider rate tables in this repo).
- Time Series Forecasting: ARIMA, Prophet methodologies

- [ ] Failure mode E2E validates actionable recovery UI/messages (with artifacts captured)

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + window + recordCount + computeTimeMs; individual prompts are never logged

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-toe","title":"Git Coordination Service","description":"## Overview

The Git Coordination Service provides centralized management of git operations across multiple agents working in the same repository. This is critical for Phase 3 Flywheel Integration where multiple AI agents may be simultaneously modifying code.

## Background & Reasoning

When multiple agents operate on a shared codebase, git coordination becomes essential to prevent:
- Merge conflicts from overlapping changes
- Lost work from force pushes
- Branch naming collisions
- Inconsistent repository state

The Git Coordination Service acts as a single source of truth for branch assignments and provides predictive conflict detection before agents begin work.

## Technical Architecture

### Core Components

1. **Branch Assignment Manager**
   - Maintains registry of agent-to-branch mappings
   - Enforces exclusive branch ownership
   - Supports branch reservation with TTL
   - Handles branch release on agent completion/timeout

2. **Conflict Prediction Engine**
   - Analyzes pending changes across all active branches
   - Detects overlapping file modifications before they occur
   - Uses git diff-tree for file-level conflict detection
   - Provides semantic conflict hints (same function modified)

3. **Sync Operations Manager**
   - Coordinates pull operations with conflict checking
   - Manages commit sequencing for dependent changes
   - Handles push operations with retry logic
   - Supports atomic multi-branch operations

4. **Merge Base Analyzer**
   - Tracks merge base for all active branches
   - Calculates branch divergence metrics
   - Recommends rebase timing
   - Detects stale branches requiring attention

### Frontend Components

1. **BranchVisualization.tsx**
   - D3.js-based git graph rendering
   - Real-time branch status updates via WebSocket
   - Interactive branch selection and comparison
   - Commit history timeline

2. **ConflictPredictor.tsx**
   - Visual file overlap detection
   - Agent workspace comparison
   - Suggested resolution strategies

3. **SyncStatusPanel.tsx**
   - Per-agent sync status indicators
   - Push/pull operation progress
   - Error state visualization

## API Design

```typescript
interface GitCoordinationService {
  // Branch Management
  assignBranch(agentId: string, branchName: string): Promise<BranchAssignment>;
  releaseBranch(agentId: string): Promise<void>;
  getBranchAssignments(): Promise<BranchAssignment[]>;
  
  // Conflict Prediction
  predictConflicts(branchA: string, branchB: string): Promise<ConflictPrediction>;
  getOverlappingFiles(branches: string[]): Promise<FileOverlapReport>;
  
  // Sync Operations
  syncBranch(agentId: string, operation: SyncOperation): Promise<SyncResult>;
  getMergeBase(branch: string, target: string): Promise<MergeBaseInfo>;
  
  // Visualization Data
  getGitGraph(options: GraphOptions): Promise<GitGraphData>;
}

interface BranchAssignment {
  agentId: string;
  branchName: string;
  assignedAt: Date;
  expiresAt?: Date;
  status: 'active' | 'stale' | 'merged';
}

interface ConflictPrediction {
  hasConflicts: boolean;
  conflictingFiles: string[];
  severity: 'none' | 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
  recommendation: string;
}
```

## File Locations

- `apps/gateway/src/services/git.service.ts` - Core coordination service
- `apps/gateway/src/services/git-conflict.service.ts` - Conflict prediction logic
- `apps/gateway/src/services/git-sync.service.ts` - Sync operations
- `apps/web/src/components/git/BranchVisualization.tsx` - Git graph component
- `apps/web/src/components/git/ConflictPredictor.tsx` - Conflict UI
- `apps/web/src/components/git/SyncStatusPanel.tsx` - Sync status display
- `apps/web/src/components/git/BranchAssignmentList.tsx` - Agent-branch mapping UI

## Database Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE branch_assignments (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  agent_id UUID REFERENCES agents(id),
  branch_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  repository_id UUID REFERENCES repositories(id),
  assigned_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  expires_at TIMESTAMP,
  status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'active',
  UNIQUE(repository_id, branch_name)
);

CREATE TABLE conflict_predictions (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  branch_a VARCHAR(255),
  branch_b VARCHAR(255),
  conflicting_files JSONB,
  severity VARCHAR(50),
  predicted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Agents can request and receive exclusive branch assignments
- [ ] Branch assignments expire after configurable TTL
- [ ] Conflict prediction identifies overlapping file changes with >95% accuracy
- [ ] Real-time git graph updates within 500ms of changes
- [ ] Sync operations handle transient failures with exponential backoff
- [ ] Branch visualization supports repositories with 1000+ commits
- [ ] All git operations are logged for audit trail
- [ ] WebSocket events notify clients of branch status changes

## Testing Requirements

- Unit tests for conflict prediction algorithms
- Integration tests for git operations (using test repositories)
- E2E tests for branch assignment workflow
- Performance tests for large repository handling
- Chaos testing for concurrent agent operations

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Repo state detection: clean/dirty/diverged/conflicted
- [ ] Safety policy checks for destructive git operations (gated)

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Run git coordination actions against a fixture repo and validate emitted events
- [ ] Conflict detection produces actionable diagnostics

### E2E Tests
- [ ] UI: run a safe coordination action (status) across repos and view results
- [ ] UI: simulate conflict and verify conflict workflow is triggered

### Logging
- [ ] Git-coordination tests log repo path/id, branch, commit IDs, and job IDs with correlation IDs
- [ ] Safety failures produce actionable logs without leaking credentials/remote URLs


## Security Considerations

- Branch assignments scoped to authenticated agents only
- Git credentials managed via secure credential store
- Audit logging for all git write operations
- Rate limiting on sync operations

## References

- PLAN.md §18 - Git Coordination Architecture
- Git merge-base documentation
- libgit2 bindings for Node.js","notes":"## Testing Requirements

- [ ] Conflict prediction under 500ms for 100 file changes
- [ ] Memory usage bounded for large repositories
